reasoning: The document has high LLM fidelity scores, but programmatic detectors identified 4 major structural and accessibility defects. These include retained header/footer text, a list broken across a page break, misclassified Roman numeral lists, and truncated alt text. Based on the count of 4 major defects, this document requires human review.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (4): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 4; Page 3 of 4
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=4, pages_affected=4/4. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p3→p4: list item ends “…nditions at these sites do not present a”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 1, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=1. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (2 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...g a large green 'N' and dark blue 'C' with a white"; p1: "...e green on the left to dark navy blue on the right"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=2, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document contains lists where the leading topic of each bullet point is visibly bolded and underlined (e.g., 'Environmental Justice', 'Risk Classification', 'Not Applicable', 'Beneficial Reuse'). The output JSON fails to capture this visual emphasis, presenting the text as plain strings without markdown bolding (**text**) or other markers.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Environmental Justice – One speaker was from Lee County...
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Environmental Justice – One speaker was from Lee County..."

action_items: Remove the retained header and footer elements from the HTML.
Rejoin the list that was split across pages 3 and 4.
Fix the list markers on the Roman numeral list.
Provide complete and descriptive alt text for the two affected images.
Restore bold and underline formatting to the leading topics in the bulleted lists.
